Your issue is not numpy-specific, you may be better served on other forums like stack overflow that are better suited to beginners. With that, you may try any of the following, in my personal order of preference:
> 
> * use anaconda/miniconda and activate an environment
> * set up and activate a virtual environment
> * always use `python -m pip install` rather than `pip install` to make sure you are consistently using the correct python environment
> * never use pip, always use a platform-level package manager like homebrew

It really works! Just use anaconda to manage the environment and modules instead by vscode and pip.
